title: The Stand-In

jokes.text: The bus is the single stupidest, fattest... slowest, most despised vehicle on the road, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Jerry's stand-up routine about buses
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top string of insults directed at buses
jokes.explanation: Classic Seinfeld observational humor with Jerry's signature style of turning mundane annoyances into elaborate rants

jokes.text: Ever notice when you get behind a bus, people go: 'What are you doing? Get away. Come on.'
jokes.setup: Continuing the bus complaint theme
jokes.punchline: Mimicking people's frustrated reactions to being stuck behind buses
jokes.explanation: Solid observational material that captures a relatable experience through voice mimicry

jokes.text: The back of the bus, it's like an eclipse, isn't it? People are just like, 'The sun. Where is the sun?'
jokes.setup: Bus blocks view like an eclipse
jokes.punchline: Exaggerated comparison to people missing the sun during an eclipse
jokes.explanation: Clever visual metaphor that escalates the bus complaint to cosmic proportions - very Seinfeld-esque

jokes.text: It's like this huge metal ass... just taking up the whole windshield of your car.
jokes.setup: Continuing the visual description of the bus
jokes.punchline: Crude comparison to anatomy that's unexpectedly vulgar
jokes.explanation: Memorable crude visual that works because of the unexpected vulgarity in an otherwise mundane observation

jokes.text: When it pulls out, it even sounds like a fat uncle trying to get out of a sofa.
jokes.setup: Bus making noise when departing
jokes.punchline: Specific, absurd comparison to overweight relative struggling with furniture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific visual comparison that's both unexpected and perfectly captures the sound - peak Seinfeld observational comedy

jokes.text: It's just not good. It's not good. - It's not good.
jokes.setup: Discussing boring relationships
jokes.punchline: Repetitive, deflated assessment that says nothing while saying everything
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of Seinfeld's minimalist repetitive dialogue that becomes funny through rhythm and delivery

jokes.text: I'm bored. She's boring, I'm boring. We're both boring.
jokes.setup: George describing his relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Escalating self-awareness of boring nature of relationship
jokes.explanation: Classic George neurotic self-analysis with escalating admission of mutual boring-ness

jokes.text: We go out to eat, read newspapers. - At breakfast, everybody reads. - No, lunch, we read. - Dinner, we read.
jokes.setup: Describing boring relationship routine
jokes.punchline: Obsessive cataloging of when they read newspapers instead of talking
jokes.explanation: George's obsessive need to categorize and Jerry's interest in the details creates comedy through their neuroses

jokes.text: At least we're talking about how there's nothing. Talk with her about how there's nothing. She knows there's nothing. - At least she'll be talking.
jokes.setup: Jerry suggesting George talk to girlfriend about having nothing to talk about
jokes.punchline: Circular logic of talking about not talking, with George's practical conclusion
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor about the paradox of discussing having nothing to discuss - very Seinfeld philosophical absurdity

jokes.text: Deeply in love. We have so many things to talk about. Sometimes, we'll talk all night... till the sun comes up.
jokes.setup: Al appears just as they're complaining about having nothing to talk about
jokes.punchline: Perfect contrast with his gushing about endless conversation with his girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing and contrast - Al's romantic bliss immediately follows their boring relationship discussion

jokes.text: We have no need to speak. We communicate with deep, soulful looks. Like Dwight and Mamie Eisenhower.
jokes.setup: Jerry describing his relationship communication style
jokes.punchline: Bizarre comparison to the famously cold presidential couple
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and historically informed comparison that's both unexpected and perfect for Jerry's character

jokes.text: Said he could use a good laugh. - What about me? He didn't mention you.
jokes.setup: Al says Fulton could use a good laugh from Jerry
jokes.punchline: George's hopeful question met with brutal honesty that he wasn't mentioned
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - his insecurity met with casual dismissal creates awkward cringe comedy

jokes.text: You can't say something bad about relationships... you shouldn't say anything at all.
jokes.setup: Jerry's reaction to Al's romantic happiness
jokes.punchline: Twist on 'if you can't say something nice' - only negative relationship talk allowed
jokes.explanation: Clever inversion of common saying that perfectly captures Jerry's cynical worldview about relationships

jokes.text: 'Could use a good laugh.' You know what pressure that is?
jokes.setup: Jerry told Fulton could use a good laugh
jokes.punchline: Jerry's neurotic anxiety about performance pressure in dire situation
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Jerry's comedian neurosis about being expected to be funny on command

jokes.text: I can't hide my pity. I make them feel worse.
jokes.setup: George explaining why he's bad with sick people
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest self-assessment of his lack of bedside manner
jokes.explanation: Perfect George character comedy - his complete lack of social skills delivered with painful self-awareness

jokes.text: Also, I'm afraid... that people in that state are finally gonna tell me what they really think. They got nothing to lose. What do they care?
jokes.setup: George continuing explanation of why he avoids sick people
jokes.punchline: Paranoid fear that dying people will be brutally honest about him
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and perfectly George - his self-centered paranoia even extends to deathbed scenarios

jokes.text: Mickey's a stand-in for an 8-year-old kid... and I stand in for the kid's father.
jokes.setup: Explaining Kramer and Mickey's jobs on soap opera
jokes.punchline: Adult little person stands in for child while tall Kramer stands in for the father
jokes.explanation: Absurd visual setup that plays on height differences and casting logic of soap operas

jokes.text: The kid I stand in for, he's growing. He was 4 feet last month. Now he's like 4'2" and a half. He shot up 2 and a half inches.
jokes.setup: Kramer explaining his work problem
jokes.punchline: Obsessive tracking of child actor's growth with precise measurements
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer character comedy - his obsessive attention to bizarre details of his odd job situation

jokes.text: I can do 4'2", 4'3" is a stretch. Any higher, I'm gonna be out on my ass... doing that paralegal crap.
jokes.setup: Kramer's height limitations for his stand-in job
jokes.punchline: Treating one-inch height difference as career-ending, plus 'stretch' pun and disdain for paralegal work
jokes.explanation: Multi-layered joke with height pun, absurd precision, and Kramer's casual dismissal of normal careers

jokes.text: How do you stop a kid from growing? I told you, you should offer him some cigarettes.
jokes.setup: Kramer's problem with growing child actor
jokes.punchline: Mickey's matter-of-fact suggestion to stunt growth with cigarettes
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny and shocking solution delivered matter-of-factly - perfectly inappropriate and memorable

jokes.text: I offered him cigarettes... but his stupid mother's always hanging around. She won't let him have any.
jokes.setup: Following up on cigarette suggestion
jokes.punchline: Kramer actually tried it and is frustrated by responsible parenting
jokes.explanation: Escalates the dark humor by revealing Kramer actually attempted child endangerment and blames the mother - peak Kramer logic

jokes.text: Can't you just switch with another midget? It's 'little people.' - You got that? - Easy, Mickey. Easy.
jokes.setup: Jerry's innocent question about changing assignments
jokes.punchline: Mickey's sharp correction about terminology and Kramer calming him down
jokes.explanation: Standard PC language correction comedy with Mickey's sensitivity and Kramer's diplomatic intervention

jokes.text: You gotta get some lifts for your shoes. Lifts? Kramer, you don't understand. This kind of thing is just not done.
jokes.setup: Kramer suggesting height enhancement
jokes.punchline: Mickey treating shoe lifts as violation of sacred code
jokes.explanation: Sets up Mickey's ethical dilemma and foreshadows the heightening scandal subplot

jokes.text: I could use a good laugh. - Who couldn't? - I haven't cracked a smile in months. - Oh, don't worry. You'll crack.
jokes.setup: Fulton asking for laughs, admitting he hasn't smiled
jokes.punchline: Jerry's overconfident assurance using 'crack' double meaning
jokes.explanation: Jerry's comedian confidence with wordplay on 'crack' - shows his obliviousness to the pressure

jokes.text: Cracking's inevitable. First you crack, then you chuckle. That was the Russians' motto at the Siege of Leningrad.
jokes.setup: Jerry's philosophy about laughter being inevitable
jokes.punchline: Absurdly attributing this optimistic motto to Russians during horrific historical event
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor combining Jerry's clueless optimism with historically inappropriate reference - very sophisticated dark comedy

jokes.text: You know, because Leningrad... when the Nazis attacked, it wasn't a very happy time. Because of the war. Famine. Plus, it was cold. Very cold. They were eating each other.
jokes.setup: Jerry realizing his historical reference was inappropriate
jokes.punchline: Digging deeper by explaining increasingly horrible details of the siege
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe escalation as Jerry makes it exponentially worse by over-explaining - masterful uncomfortable comedy

jokes.text: So how are the eggs? Eggs are eggs.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about Elaine's breakfast
jokes.punchline: Elaine's matter-of-fact tautological response
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy of language - Elaine's practical philosophy delivered with deadpan efficiency

jokes.text: That is very profound. By the same token, could you say, 'Fish is fish'? I don't think so.
jokes.setup: Jerry treating 'eggs are eggs' as philosophical statement
jokes.punchline: Analyzing the logic of tautologies with different foods
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry over-analysis of mundane statements - his philosophical obsession with language patterns

jokes.text: Al Nechy called me today. He told me not to get involved with you. He said you could never make a commitment... and you'd just wind up hurting me.
jokes.setup: Elaine reporting Al's warning about Jerry
jokes.punchline: Al's accurate assessment delivered just as Jerry was about to prove it true
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony - Al's warning arrives precisely when Jerry was planning to break up

jokes.text: I mean, sure, there may have been one or two occasions in the past... when I may have reacted... in an impulsive or somewhat immature manner... but those days are well behind me.
jokes.setup: Jerry denying Al's commitment accusations
jokes.punchline: Elaborate euphemistic confession that proves Al's point
jokes.explanation: Jerry's elaborate self-justification that actually confirms his relationship failures - good character consistency

jokes.text: How long are you gonna be away for, Daddy? I'm not really going away. I told you, I'll be back every other weekend. Don't go, Daddy. Don't go.
jokes.setup: Soap opera scene about divorce
jokes.punchline: Melodramatic father-son goodbye scene being performed
jokes.explanation: Standard soap opera parody - establishes the dramatic context that Kramer and Mickey are standing in for

jokes.text: How do those lifts feel? Quiet.
jokes.setup: Kramer asking about Mickey's secret shoe lifts
jokes.punchline: Mickey's paranoid one-word response indicating shame and secrecy
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character beat - Mickey's guilt over heightening expressed in single paranoid word

jokes.text: You look different. - Have you been working out? - Not that I know of. Whatever it is, keep doing it. You look great.
jokes.setup: Tammy noticing Mickey looks different
jokes.punchline: Mickey's denial while benefiting from the lifts he's ashamed of
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Mickey gets positive attention from the thing he's ashamed of, while denying it

jokes.text: There's something different about you. - I got my hair cut, that's all. - No, that's not it. Something else. You don't. You got the same ugly mug you had since the day I met you.
jokes.setup: Johnny suspicious of Mickey's change
jokes.punchline: Johnny's brutal honesty about Kramer's unchanged ugly face
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and character insult - Johnny's investigation leads to random Kramer insult

jokes.text: So he picks up the two pieces of pizza and... And they're steaming hot. They're burning his hands, so he's juggling. He's throwing them in the air. And just as he gets up to her, down they go.
jokes.setup: Jerry telling the Pachyderm pizza story to Fulton
jokes.punchline: Detailed description of physical comedy mishap
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy story - sets up the Pachyderm pizza incident that becomes important later

jokes.text: Octane, butane, night train!
jokes.setup: Fulton supposedly cheered up by Jerry's visit
jokes.punchline: Fulton's nonsensical outburst suggesting mental deterioration
jokes.explanation: Darkly absurd moment showing Jerry's visit may have made things worse - memorable random phrase

jokes.text: If I was a woman, I'd be at the dock waiting for the fleet to come in.
jokes.setup: Asked if he'd date Phil if he were a woman
jokes.punchline: Jerry's crude admission he'd be promiscuous as a woman
jokes.explanation: Memorable crude visual and honest character revelation - classic Jerry inappropriate honesty

jokes.text: He's afraid that she's gonna get hurt. - Is she? - Of course.
jokes.setup: George asking if Al was right about Jerry hurting women
jokes.punchline: Jerry's matter-of-fact confirmation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry moment - casual admission of his relationship destructiveness with no shame

jokes.text: So now you're gonna keep going out with her for spite? - Yes. I am. - Yeah, I could see that. - I don't see any way around it. - Me either. - What choice do I have? - None.
jokes.setup: George understanding Jerry's twisted logic
jokes.punchline: Both characters agreeing this makes perfect sense
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy showing both characters' twisted logic - they genuinely believe this insane reasoning

jokes.text: Fulton's wife told me it's all my fault. She said since my visit, he's taken a turn for the worse.
jokes.setup: Jerry reporting on Fulton's condition
jokes.punchline: Jerry's comedy visit actually made sick man worse
jokes.explanation: Dark irony of comedian's visit making patient worse - callback to Jerry's performance anxiety

jokes.text: When my friend Len Nickidimo had gout... I was in his hospital room for three days. The doctors were amazed at his recovery.
jokes.setup: George claiming to be good with sick people
jokes.punchline: Specific story with precise name and miraculous healing claim
jokes.explanation: Perfect George delusion with hyper-specific details - 'Len Nickidimo' is particularly good invented name

jokes.text: Johnny Viggiano went through my locker. That little bastard. He saw the lifts in my shoes. He knows I'm heightening.
jokes.setup: Mickey's paranoia about being discovered
jokes.punchline: Treating 'heightening' as serious scandal with its own terminology
jokes.explanation: Great escalation with 'heightening' as scandal - Mickey's paranoia and new terminology works well

jokes.text: This never would've happened if you didn't push me to get those. - I told you. - Nobody put a gun to your head. Yeah. Just keep out of my business, you big ape. Who you calling 'big ape'? I'm calling you an ape.
jokes.setup: Mickey blaming Kramer for the lifts idea
jokes.punchline: Argument escalating to little person calling tall person 'big ape'
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with height-based insult reversal - Mickey's anger making him call Kramer an ape

jokes.text: He's always been jealous of me. I always got to stand in for the bigger stars. The Cosby kids, Ricky Schroder, Macaulay Culkin.
jokes.setup: Mickey explaining Johnny's jealousy
jokes.punchline: Mickey's pride in standing in for famous child actors
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mickey's professional pride and status hierarchy among little people actors

jokes.text: There's an unwritten code about this. I could be ostracized. I remember when I was a kid, some guy tried to heighten. He lost his job, lost his friends, everything.
jokes.setup: Mickey explaining the seriousness of heightening
jokes.punchline: Treating shoe lifts as career-ending scandal with tragic precedent
jokes.explanation: Brilliant worldbuilding - creates entire ethical system around height enhancement with tragic backstory

jokes.text: He bought a new blanket. And he got that game with the foam paddles and the Velcro ball.
jokes.setup: George's lie about father-son picnic
jokes.punchline: Hyper-specific details about father's picnic preparations
jokes.explanation: Good George lying technique - overly specific details to make lie more believable

jokes.text: I don't know what he's gonna do with all that potato salad.
jokes.setup: George canceling fake picnic plans
jokes.punchline: Adding more unnecessary detail to maintain the lie
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of George's lie escalation - adding emotional manipulation about wasted potato salad

jokes.text: You really are beautiful, Elaine. Well... - Good night. - Good night?
jokes.setup: Phil complimenting Elaine after good date
jokes.punchline: Elaine's surprised reaction to his early departure
jokes.explanation: Setup for the 'he took it out' revelation - creates false expectation before the shocking twist

jokes.text: He took it out. He what? He took... it out.
jokes.setup: Jerry asking about Elaine's date
jokes.punchline: Elaine's shocking revelation delivered with euphemistic phrasing
jokes.explanation: Legendary Seinfeld moment - shocking, perfectly delivered, became cultural touchstone. Economy of language with maximum impact.

jokes.text: You were involved in some sort of amorous... No. - You mean, he just...? - Yes. - Are you sure? - Oh, quite.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to understand what happened
jokes.punchline: Elaine's confirmation that it was completely unprovoked
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the shock - Jerry's disbelief and Elaine's dignified confirmation builds the comedy

jokes.text: So you were talking, you're having a pleasant conversation... and then all of a sudden... - Yeah. - It. - It. - Out. - Out.
jokes.setup: Jerry trying to process the timeline
jokes.punchline: Rhythmic callback to the key phrases with building emphasis
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback structure building rhythm and emphasis - classic Seinfeld dialogue pattern that enhances the original joke

jokes.text: He took it out. Well, maybe it needed some air. You know, sometimes they need air. They can't breathe in there. It's inhuman.
jokes.setup: Elaine telling Kramer about Phil
jokes.punchline: Kramer's matter-of-fact physiological explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer response - completely earnest physiological explanation for inappropriate behavior. Peak character comedy.

jokes.text: I spend time trying to get their clothes off... I never thought of taking mine off.
jokes.setup: George learning about Phil's direct approach
jokes.punchline: George's realization about different seduction strategies
jokes.explanation: Great George character moment - his analytical approach to learning from Phil's 'technique'

jokes.text: How long are you gonna keep this up? Hey, I'll get married if I have to.
jokes.setup: George asking about Jerry's spite relationship
jokes.punchline: Jerry's extreme commitment to proving Al wrong
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Jerry's twisted logic - willing to marry someone out of spite is peak Jerry behavior

jokes.text: I don't know what I did wrong. Well, you showed her who you are.
jokes.setup: Phil confused about why date went badly
jokes.punchline: Jerry's brutally honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response - Jerry's matter-of-fact brutality disguised as philosophical wisdom

jokes.text: What, she's gotta breast-feed in public? Yeah, that's the last thing you wanna see. Well, next to last.
jokes.setup: Jerry complaining about public breastfeeding
jokes.punchline: Phil's reference to his own inappropriate exposure
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure - Phil's subtle reference to his own exposure incident

jokes.text: Rock, paper, scissors. Match. - Rock, paper, scissors. Match. All right. Rock beats paper. I thought paper covered rock. No. Rock flies right through paper.
jokes.setup: Playing rock paper scissors
jokes.punchline: Kramer's completely wrong understanding of how the game works
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer logic - confidently wrong explanation with vivid physical imagery

jokes.text: Well, what beats rock? Nothing beats rock.
jokes.setup: Mickey questioning Kramer's rock paper scissors rules
jokes.punchline: Kramer's declaration that rock is unbeatable
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of Kramer's flawed logic - creates invincible rock scenario

jokes.text: Everybody knows that you're heightening. It's all over the set. All the progress we've made over the years... and you blow it with a stupid stunt like that.
jokes.setup: Mickey's heightening scandal exposed
jokes.punchline: Treating shoe lifts as betrayal of civil rights progress
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the heightening scandal to civil rights level - absurd but consistent with established world

jokes.text: I'm Mickey Abbot. I stood in for Punky Brewster when all of you was nothing.
jokes.setup: Mickey defending himself against heightening accusations
jokes.punchline: Appeal to his professional credentials and past glory
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Mickey's pride and desperation appealing to his Punky Brewster credentials

jokes.text: Fulton?
jokes.setup: Jerry finishing his comedy routine for Fulton
jokes.punchline: Awkward silence suggesting Fulton may have died during the performance
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy beat - Jerry's comedy may have literally killed someone. Maximum impact with minimal dialogue.

jokes.text: Jerry Persach. Pachyderm? He was carrying these two pieces of pizza...
jokes.setup: Daphne revealing she met someone at a bar
jokes.punchline: It's Pachyderm, the subject of Jerry's hospital story
jokes.explanation: Perfect circular structure - the pizza story that was meant to cheer Fulton ends up breaking up George's relationship

tmdb_overview: George postpones breaking up with his girlfriend; Kramer befriends a fellow soap-opera stand-in; and Jerry bombs trying to cheer up a sick friend.
